lessons: Fed stress test catalyst pattern: When the Fed announces significant Stress Capital Buffer reductions enabling dividend increases, financial sector stocks can sustain elevated RSI levels (80+) because new income-seeking buyers enter at the higher yield. Do not exit purely on overbought RSI — wait for OBV to turn negative before exiting a catalyst-driven move.

First-review deployment discipline: In the initial portfolio with no prior market context, deploy 30-40% maximum across 3-4 highest-conviction ideas only. Remaining cash is optionality, not underperformance — the next 2-3 reviews will reveal the macro regime and enable bolder deployment with better information.

lessons: Bilateral trade deal risk for tariff plays: When holding domestic commodity tariff beneficiary positions (copper, steel producers), actively monitor for bilateral US trade deal announcements with major trading partners (EU, China, Canada). Invalidation of the monitoring need: if the deal explicitly excludes the relevant commodity category.

belief_updates.name: OBV primacy: track institutional flow, not price momentum
belief_updates.description: In catalyst-driven markets — Fed stress tests, AI chip policy shifts, earnings beats — RSI becomes unreliable as an exit signal because new institutional buyers enter at elevated prices, sustaining overbought readings for weeks. OBV is the authoritative signal for institutional behavior: rising OBV = accumulation regardless of RSI level; falling OBV = distribution regardless of thesis quality. Rule: never exit a position purely on overbought RSI when OBV is rising. Always trigger an exit review when OBV turns negative, even when the fundamental thesis remains intact. A price dip with OBV rising is a buy signal, not an exit.

lessons: Recovery-and-distribute exit pattern: When a position experiences a significant drawdown but recovers to near-entry or above-entry while OBV simultaneously turns negative, that price recovery is the optimal exit window — not a signal to hold for further gains. The combination of (1) price recovery to entry zone + (2) OBV falling + (3) weak trend ADX < 25 + (4) near upper BB is a high-conviction exit signal even when the fundamental thesis remains superficially intact. Trigger: any position showing this four-factor combination. Action: exit promptly into the recovery rather than waiting for OBV distribution to manifest in price decline.

lessons: Structural decline short fundamental reversal rule: When a short thesis is anchored to sustained revenue decline (e.g., CMCSA thesis assumed -6.4% RevGr as the structural condition), verify the revenue trend at each quarterly fundamentals update. A single quarter of revenue stabilization or recovery to positive growth is sufficient to invalidate the thesis — markets price the reversal forward, not backward. Trigger: any short position where quarterly RevGr improves from negative to flat or positive. Action: cover the short within the current review cycle without waiting for OBV confirmation. The fundamental premise — not the technical signal — is the primary invalidation condition for structural shorts. Invalidation of this monitoring need: revenue immediately reverses back negative the following quarter.

lessons: Leveraged single-stock ETF launch as peak retail enthusiasm signal: When a financial product issuer launches leveraged single-stock ETFs targeting a specific name (e.g., Defiance launching leveraged ETFs on SMCI, PLTR, AMD simultaneously), this creates two distinct effects. (1) Short-term mechanical buying: daily rebalancing of leveraged long ETFs requires purchasing the underlying, creating price support. (2) Medium-term contrarian warning: leveraged ETF launches on volatile, high-beta names historically mark the transition from institutional to retail ownership — the product is being created because retail demand for the story exists at peak enthusiasm. Trigger: news of leveraged single-stock ETF launch on a name already down 10%+ from highs (like SMCI, PLTR). Action for longs: evaluate whether thesis is fundamentals-driven or momentum-driven — if fundamentals remain strong (PLTR 32.6% margins), hold but tighten mental stops as retail now owns the story; if margins are thin (SMCI 3.4%), the retail peak is a short entry confirmation. Action for shorts: leveraged ETF launch on a technically broken name (below SMA50, OBV falling) is a high-conviction short entry signal as institutional exit is being masked by mechanical retail buying.

lessons: Earnings beat + pre-existing OBV decline = distribution into strength, not accumulation. When a high-profile AI infrastructure name (e.g., NVDA) reports a quarter that confirms the secular thesis — including landmark capex forecasts from hyperscalers — but OBV was already falling in the days/weeks before the earnings release, institutions are using the positive event to distribute into retail enthusiasm, not accumulate. The beat temporarily attracts retail buyers who absorb institutional selling volume. This is the institutional 'sell the news' mechanism. Trigger: OBV falling for 1-2 weeks before a major earnings release on a stock that has already run up significantly into the print. Action: do not add to position on the earnings beat; treat the beat as a potential exit window if OBV continues falling post-release. Monitor OBV for 2 weeks post-earnings. Invalidation: OBV reverses to rising within 2 weeks of earnings, indicating institutions are re-accumulating on the news.

lessons: EU antitrust fine absorption pattern: When the EU imposes a large antitrust fine on a US megacap tech company, monitor three factors before acting: (1) fine size relative to market cap — if under 2%, it is unlikely to materially impair the investment thesis; (2) US government posture toward the fine — active executive branch opposition signals a political backstop that limits further escalation; (3) OBV behavior in the 48 hours after the announcement — if OBV remains rising, institutions are treating the fine as a non-event and absorbing it. When all three factors are benign, hold the position. Trigger: EU fine announced on a tech holding. Action: evaluate fine-to-market-cap ratio, check executive branch statements, watch OBV for 48 hours. If OBV turns negative within 48 hours of the fine announcement, reduce by 30% as institutions may be pricing sequential regulatory risk.

lessons: Operational disaster as exit confirmation in multi-signal deteriorating positions: When a commodity or industrial company has already triggered 3+ exit signals (OBV falling, below SMA50, MACD bearish, near lower BB), a subsequent operational disaster news item (mine collapse, mud rush, facility incident) is not incremental information to 'wait and assess' — it is confirmation to exit immediately at next available price. The technical signals indicated institutional distribution was already in progress before the public news broke; sophisticated sellers knew or anticipated the operational risk. Waiting for damage assessment or guidance update means selling after the second wave of selling completes. Trigger: operational disaster news on a position that has already triggered 3+ exit signals with OBV falling. Action: exit on same day without waiting for company response or guidance. Invalidation: position has only 1-2 exit signals total and OBV is still positive (operational news may be isolated and absorbable).
